<!-- headroom:learn:start -->
## Headroom Learned Patterns
*Auto-generated by `headroom learn` on 2026-07-01 — do not edit manually*

### Rust-OS: Large/hot files — use grep/sed, not full Read
*~43,596 tokens/session saved*
- `src/memory.rs`, `src/security.rs`, `src/syscall/mod.rs`, `glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/ffi.rs`, `src/net/tcp.rs`, `src/net/ip.rs`, and `glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/gtype.rs` are large, frequently-edited files that get Read repeatedly (up to 35x in one session) because prior reads get invalidated by concurrent edits.
- Prefer `grep -n` / `sed -n 'X,Yp'` for targeted lookups on these files instead of re-Reading the whole file each time content is needed.

### Rust-OS: Agent worktree isolation
*~43,596 tokens/session saved*
- Subagents/teammates spawned in `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` cannot Write to the shared checkout path (`/Users/justinadams/Downloads/Rust-OS/...`) — every attempt errors with 'This agent is isolated in the worktree'.
- When delegating a file-write task to an isolated agent, tell it up front to `ls`/target-write inside `.claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/...` (mirroring the shared repo path), not the shared path, to avoid the write→error→ls→rewrite loop seen in nearly every glib-native module-porting session.
- New files created in a worktree are NOT visible under the shared repo path — `grep`/`ls` against the shared path for a just-created worktree file will return nothing; check the worktree copy.

### Rust-OS: Cargo build/test invocation
*~12,000 tokens/session saved*
- The nested `.cargo/config.toml` files (root `Rust-OS/.cargo/config.toml` and `glib-rust/glib/rust/.cargo/config.toml`) set a JSON target-spec + `build-std`, which breaks plain `cargo test`/`cargo check` in `glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native` with `.json target specs require -Zjson-target-spec` or `error: target was empty`.
- Working invocation for glib-native tests: `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=false CARGO_BUILD_TARGET="" cargo +stable test --target aarch64-apple-darwin -p glib-native`. For the kernel binary: `cargo +nightly check --bin rustos -Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc -Zjson-target-spec --target <path-to-json-target>`.
- Do not retry bare `cargo test`/`cargo check` in this repo — it always fails the same way; go straight to the invocation above.

### Rust-OS: Edit-before-read on lib.rs
*~11,794 tokens/session saved*
- `glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/lib.rs` and similar registry files repeatedly trigger 'File has not been modified since read' / 'File has not been read yet' errors on Edit — always Read the file immediately before Editing it in the same turn, especially after another tool call (Write/Bash) may have touched it.

### Large File Reading — Also Applies Outside glib-native
*~90,000 tokens/session saved*
- Read tool truncates mid-file (returns `runtime_error` showing partial content) on large files throughout `src/`, not just `glib-native`. Confirmed large/truncating files: `src/memory.rs`, `src/security.rs`, `src/syscall/mod.rs`, `src/net/tcp.rs`, `src/net/ip.rs`, `src/net/dns.rs`, `src/linux_compat/process_ops.rs`, `src/linux_compat/advanced_io.rs`, `src/linux_compat/socket_ops.rs`, `src/elf_loader/parser.rs`, `src/fs/ext2.rs`/`ext4.rs`, `src/vfs/drmfs.rs`, `src/drivers/clk/mod.rs`, `glib-rust/.../src/ffi.rs`.
- Use `grep -n` to find the target line range, then `sed -n '<start>,<end>p'` — never `Read` these whole; repeated whole-file Reads on the same path within a session (seen 8-35x on single files) are the single largest token-waste pattern in this repo.

### Cargo Test Loop — Batch Verification
*~15,000 tokens/session saved*
- The `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=false CARGO_BUILD_TARGET="" cargo +stable test --target aarch64-apple-darwin` command was re-run 69 times in one session, most after single-line edits — each run costs ~2-4K tokens even on success.
- Batch multiple module edits (or at minimum fix all known compile errors from the last error list) before re-running the full test suite, instead of testing after every individual Edit.

### Edit Staleness — Read Immediately Before Every Edit
*~11,794 tokens/session saved*
- "File has not been read yet" and "File has been modified since read" Edit errors recur constantly on `lib.rs`, `rust-migration.md`, `gtask.rs`, `gloadableicon.rs`, `gfileicon.rs`, and non-glib files like `src/main.rs` — not limited to glib-native.
- Always `Read` the exact target file (worktree copy if isolated) in the same turn immediately before `Edit`, even if it was read earlier in the conversation; parallel agents and prior edits invalidate cached reads.

### Shell / grep — ugrep Escaping (repo-wide, not just glib-native)
*~4,000 tokens/session saved*
- `ugrep`'s `\|` alternation syntax fails with `empty (sub)expression` / `invalid syntax` errors across the whole repo (seen in `src/` bug-sweep sessions too, not only glib-native). Always use `-E` with plain `|`: `grep -E 'pattern1|pattern2'`.
- `wc`, `awk` are unavailable inside `eval`-context shell loops (`for ... do ... done` one-liners) — use `python3 -c` for counting/line-range logic instead.

### Large File Reading — Use grep/sed for glib-native Sources
*~1,200 tokens/session saved*
Read tool truncates mid-file on files >5KB in glib-native: `gaction.rs`, `gcancellable.rs`, `ginputstream.rs`, `goutputstream.rs`, `gfilterinputstream.rs`, `gfilteroutputstream.rs`, `lib.rs`, and others. Always use `grep -n` to locate line ranges, then `sed -n '<start>,<end>p'` to extract. Never Read these files whole.

### Build Environment — Cargo Flags Required
*~800 tokens/session saved*
Always prefix `cargo` commands with `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=false CARGO_BUILD_TARGET=""` or use `cargo +stable --target aarch64-apple-darwin`. The root `.cargo/config.toml` sets a JSON build target that breaks stable cargo; these flags override it. Without them, cargo fails with 'target specs require -Zjson-target-spec'.

### Worktree Isolation — Dependency File Copies
*~600 tokens/session saved*
Agents in worktrees cannot read files from main checkout. When a new module depends on existing files (e.g., `gtlscertificate.rs`), explicitly `cp /Users/justinadams/Downloads/Rust-OS/glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/<dep>.rs <worktree>/glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/` before building or grep-searching. Otherwise module compilation fails with 'file not found for module'.

### lib.rs Volatility — Always Re-read Before Edit
*~500 tokens/session saved*
In glib-native, `lib.rs` (~27KB) is frequently edited by parallel agents. Edit fails with 'File has been modified since read' if stale. Always `Read lib.rs` immediately before Edit, even if you read it 5 minutes earlier. In worktrees, read and edit the worktree copy (`/Users/justinadams/Downloads/Rust-OS/.claude/worktrees/<agent-id>/...lib.rs`), not the main checkout.

### Shell Regex Syntax — ugrep Instead of GNU grep
*~400 tokens/session saved*
System uses `ugrep` not GNU grep. Do NOT use backslash escapes: `\|` for alternation fails with 'empty (sub)expression'. Use `-E` flag with modern syntax: `grep -E 'pattern1|pattern2'`. Never escape parentheses in character classes; use `grep -E 'pub fn|pub struct'` not `grep 'pub fn\|pub struct'`.

### Cargo / Build Commands
*~900 tokens/session saved*
- Plain `cargo test` / `cargo check` fails with `.json target specs require -Zjson-target-spec` — the root `.cargo/config.toml` sets a JSON build target that breaks stable cargo.
- Working test command for `glib-native`: `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=false CARGO_BUILD_TARGET="" cargo +stable test --target aarch64-apple-darwin`
- Working check command: `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=false CARGO_BUILD_TARGET="" cargo +stable check --target aarch64-apple-darwin`

### Large Files — glib-native
*~700 tokens/session saved*
- Many `glib-native` `.rs` files exceed the Read tool limit; the tool returns a `runtime_error` showing file content mid-file instead of an EOF error. Use `grep -n` or `sed -n '<start>,<end>p'` for these.
- Known large files requiring grep/sed: `gsocketaddress.rs`, `ginetsocketaddress.rs`, `gunixsocketaddress.rs`, `ginputstream.rs`, `goutputstream.rs`, `gcancellable.rs`, `gfilterinputstream.rs`, `gfilteroutputstream.rs`, `gaction.rs`, `gfileenumerator.rs`, `gvolume.rs`, `gmount.rs`, `gsettings.rs`, `gicon.rs`, `gloadableicon.rs`, `gfileicon.rs`.
- `lib.rs` (~27 KB+) sometimes hits the limit too; use `grep -n 'pub mod'` or `grep -n 'pub use'` to find module declarations and insertion points.

### Worktree Isolation
*~500 tokens/session saved*
- Agents launched with `isolation: 'worktree'` cannot write to the main checkout. All Writes/Edits must target the worktree copy: `/Users/justinadams/Downloads/Rust-OS/.claude/worktrees/<agent-id>/glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/<file>.rs`
- Worktrees do NOT auto-copy existing source files. When a new module depends on existing files (e.g., `gtlscertificate.rs` for TLS work), explicitly `cp` them before building: `cp /Users/justinadams/Downloads/Rust-OS/glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/<dep>.rs <worktree>/glib-rust/glib/rust/glib-native/src/`

### GLib-Native Module Pattern
*~350 tokens/session saved*
- Every new module needs two steps: (1) write `src/<modname>.rs`, (2) add `pub mod <modname>;` and `pub use <modname>::{...};` to `lib.rs`. Always Read `lib.rs` immediately before editing it — it changes frequently between parallel agents and Edit fails if the file is stale.
- When working in a worktree, read and edit the worktree copy of `lib.rs`, not the main checkout copy.

### Shell / grep
*~200 tokens/session saved*
- The system uses `ugrep` not GNU grep. The `\|` alternation syntax causes `ugrep: error: empty (sub)expression`. Use `-E` with `|`: `grep -E 'pattern1|pattern2'` — never `grep 'pattern1\|pattern2'`.
- `wc` and `awk` are unavailable in `eval` contexts inside shell for-loops. Use `python3 -c` for line counting across multiple files.

<!-- headroom:learn:end -->
